message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "STEP 1 (render-half P1 CLOSED): built src/polaris_graph/outline/verified_compute.py (run_verified_compute + ComputedClaim) and added OutlineWorkspace.quantified_models registry. A number the agent DERIVES now renders in outline text ONLY via a [#calc:model:hash:field] token. Proven end-to-end on the real FinanceBench Adobe case (operating income change FY2015->FY2016): $590,507,000.00 renders through strict_verify CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0, and the kept sentence cites BOTH input spans.",
    "STEP 1 drop-guard PROVEN: the SAME derived number routed via [#ev:ev_2016:0-N] (the CITE/evidence-span path) is DROPPED by strict_verify (number_not_in_any_cited_span). Also proved a wrong number adjacent to a real calc token drops (calc_number_mismatch) and an unfaithful input fails-closed (empty registry). 5 real tests, no verifier mocks.",
    "STEP 2 (fold-in seam factored): extracted _offset_renumber + _stamp_and_delete into src/polaris_graph/outline/_fold_in.py plus a new fold_in_fetched_rows() orchestrator (url-dedup -> renumber w/ hard id-collision assert -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert). _tool_search_more_evidence now calls the shared unit; fetch_url will re-enter identically. 6 tests incl. id-collision fail-loud + two-fold disjoint-id space.",
    "STEP 3 (T1 toolkit, partial): built src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py with register_outline_toolkit() wiring 7 tools (calculator, get_evidence, search_corpus, list_baskets, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, verified_compute) onto the driver registry. Proven through the REAL agent _execute dispatch. 21 per-tool unit tests.",
    "Appended 3 dated lines to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md; committed each step."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "ea150c3",
    "e714f13",
    "4d4a6a3"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/verified_compute.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/_fold_in.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_verified_compute_render.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_fold_in_seam.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_toolkit.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "Every change run for real, not py_compile: (1) /tmp/probe_calc.py drove the Adobe case through build_quantified_spec->execute_quantified_model->strict_verify and printed CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0 + the CITE-path drop reason; (2) 46 pytest tests (5 verified_compute render, 6 fold-in seam, 21 toolkit, 14 pre-existing W3) all green; (3) verified_compute and coverage_audit driven through the real OutlineAgent._execute dispatch (client=None path) confirming registration into workspace.quantified_models and a rendered [#calc:] token.",
  "measurement": "Outline test suite went 19/19 -> 46/46 green (~2.0s). Metric (a) PROVEN with real numbers: Adobe FY2015->FY2016 operating-income delta $590,507,000.00 (real FinanceBench figures 1,493,602 - 903,095 thousands, *1000) COMPUTES via build_quantified_spec->execute_quantified_model and RENDERS via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] -> strict_verify CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0; the SAME derived number via [#ev:] -> kept=0 dropped=1 (number_not_in_any_cited_span). Metric (b): 7 T1 tools land + 21 real unit tests pass; verified_compute drives through the real _execute dispatch (rendered $1,493,602,000.00 via [#calc:m:HASH:v], model registered). Metric (c) T2 battery: NOT started this round (deferred), so S0/S1 not yet measurable.",
  "open": [
    "fetch_url NOT built yet (needs run_live_retrieval single-URL mode; will reuse the STEP-2 fold_in_fetched_rows seam).",
    "find_contradictions + corroboration_profile NOT built (NLI/basket-heavy).",
    "Decide-prompt CORE-set + categorized index scaling and ToolDefinition tags/core NOT done (redesign PART 4 step 3).",
    "T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures) NOT started -> metric (c) unmeasured.",
    "verified_compute proves arithmetic + input spans, but nothing checks the FORMULA actually answers the question (semantic correctness gap)."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The [#calc:] lane now proves a computed number's ARITHMETIC and INPUT SPANS are faithful (Adobe delta rendered, kept=1 dropped=0; derived-via-[#ev] dropped). But it does NOT prove the agent-supplied FORMULA answers the QUESTION — an agent could cite two real numbers and subtract the wrong pair, yielding a verified-but-semantically-wrong number that still renders via [#calc:]. Decision needed: for top DeepTRACE/RACE, is the current guard (fail-closed spans + material-dependency gate + two-source citation + disclosure) sufficient, or must verified_compute add a question<->formula semantic-entailment check (mirror-model, fail-open) BEFORE registering the model? And should I build fetch_url + the T2 battery next, or close find_contradictions/corroboration_profile first to unblock the contradiction battery cases (H07/H22)?



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

src/polaris_graph/retrieval/nli_benchmark_annotator.py:35:_RESIDUAL_ARTIFACT_RE = re.compile(r"\[#calc:[^\]]*\]|\(?\batom_\d+\b\)?")

1	"""The verified-compute RENDER lane for the agentic outliner (W3-render, 2026-07-11).
2	
3	This module closes the render half of the compute-reachability P1: it is the ONLY path by
4	which a number the agent DERIVED (a delta / ratio / sum) may reach rendered outline text. It
5	does so without ever widening the faithfulness surface, because it renders that number ONLY as
6	a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token — which ``strict_verify`` force-routes to
7	``verify_modeled_atom`` (provenance_generator.py:1885), NOT to the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
8	evidence-span path. A derived number can never satisfy the evidence-span path (its digits appear
9	in no single source span → ``number_not_in_any_cited_span``), so it is structurally impossible to
10	launder an unverified computed value through a citation. Proven end-to-end in
11	tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_verified_compute_render.py.
12	
13	The moat vs. frontier products: the number is not asserted, it is COMPUTED and PROVED. The tool
14	supplies {question, sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal via a datapoint match), formula intent};
15	``build_quantified_spec`` (tradeoff_modeler.py:782) validates fail-closed —
16	``literal_span_is_faithful``, the pure-arithmetic AST whitelist, exact-one datapoint match, and
17	the material-dependency perturbation gate — then ``execute_quantified_model``
18	(quantified_analysis.py:650) RE-DERIVES the number by executing the rendered (non-LLM) script in
19	the sandbox and pins its canonical ``display_value``. Regime-C verification later re-checks the
20	rendered digits against that re-execution.
21	
22	SPEND-FREE: no network, no codegen LLM. The spec is supplied by the caller and validated
23	deterministically; a bad spec returns ``None`` (the whole compute is skipped, fail-closed).
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Any, Optional
30	
31	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class ComputedClaim:
36	    """A verified, render-eligible computed number.
37	
38	    ``calc_token`` is the ONLY thing that may be emitted into outline text next to
39	    ``display_value``; ``strict_verify`` (given the workspace's ``quantified_models`` registry)
40	    keeps the sentence iff the number immediately before the token canonicalizes to exactly
41	    ``display_value`` and every sourced input resolves. Anything else about this claim is
42	    telemetry.
43	    """
44	
45	    model_id: str
46	    spec_hash: str
47	    field_id: str
48	    display_value: str
49	    calc_token: str
50	    question: str
51	    formula: str
52	
53	    def render_sentence(self, lead: str) -> str:
54	        """A minimal, verifier-passing sentence: ``<lead> <display_value> <calc_token>.``
55	
56	        ``lead`` is caller prose (e.g. "Adobe's operating income rose by"); the display value
57	        is placed IMMEDIATELY before the calc token (token adjacency, brief §1.4) so
58	        ``verify_modeled_atom`` binds the token to exactly this rendered number.
59	        """
60	        lead = lead.rstrip()
61	        return f"{lead} {self.display_value} {self.calc_token}."
62	
63	
64	async def run_verified_compute(
65	    workspace: Any,
66	    *,
67	    question: str,
68	    datapoints: list[dict[str, Any]],
69	    raw_spec: dict[str, Any],
70	    render_field: Optional[str] = None,
71	    timeout: int | None = None,
72	    on_reject: Optional[Any] = None,
73	) -> Optional[ComputedClaim]:
74	    """Build → validate → execute → REGISTER a verified quantified model; return a
75	    ``ComputedClaim`` for ONE output field (``render_field``, default: the first output).
76	
77	    Fail-closed: returns ``None`` (and discloses the reject reason on the workspace) on ANY
78	    validation or execution failure. On success, the ``QuantifiedResult`` is registered in
79	    ``workspace.quantified_models`` keyed by ``(model_id, spec_hash)`` — exactly the shape
80	    ``strict_verify(..., quantified_models=<registry>)`` consumes — so the returned claim's
81	    ``calc_token`` will verify when it is later rendered into outline text.
82	
83	    Parameters mirror the ``build_quantified_spec`` contract:
84	      * ``datapoints`` — the candidate sourced numbers (cp-format: evidence_id/label/context/
85	        value/unit); each referenced input matches EXACTLY one of these.
86	      * ``raw_spec`` — {model_id, title, inputs:[{name, datapoint_ref|modeled...}], outputs:
87	        [{name, formula, unit, display_kind}]}. The agent supplies the FORMULA INTENT here; the
88	        span derivation and re-execution are the tool's, not the agent's.
89	    """
90	    # Lazy imports: this module is loaded at agent import time; keep the heavy synthesis /
91	    # generator graph off the import path until a compute is actually requested.
92	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.quantified_analysis import execute_quantified_model  # noqa: PLC0415
93	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec  # noqa: PLC0415
94	
95	    ev_store = getattr(workspace, "ev_store", {}) or {}
96	
97	    reject_reasons: list[str] = []
98	
99	    def _reject_sink(reason: str) -> None:
100	        reject_reasons.append(reason)
101	        if on_reject is not None:
102	            try:
103	                on_reject(reason)
104	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a telemetry sink must never break the compute
105	                pass
106	
107	    def _spec_llm(_q: str, _s: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
108	        # Deterministic: the caller already produced the validated-intent spec. build_quantified_spec
109	        # still runs EVERY fail-closed gate over it (no LLM, no codegen, spend-free).
110	        return raw_spec
111	
112	    try:
113	        spec = build_quantified_spec(
114	            question, datapoints, ev_store,
115	            spec_llm=_spec_llm, on_reject=_reject_sink,
116	        )
117	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-closed: a builder defect skips the compute
118	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: spec build raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
119	        return None
120	
121	    if spec is None:
122	        _disclose(
123	            workspace,
124	            f"verified_compute: spec REJECTED (fail-closed, no number rendered) "
125	            f"reasons={reject_reasons or ['unknown']}",
126	        )
127	        return None
128	
129	    try:
130	        result = await execute_quantified_model(spec, ev_store, timeout=timeout)
131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — sandbox/exec failure is a clean skip
132	        _disclose(workspace, f"verified_compute: execution raised ({str(exc)[:120]}) — no number rendered")
133	        return None
134	
135	    if result is None:
136	        _disclose(workspace, "verified_compute: model did not execute cleanly — no number rendered (fail-closed)")
137	        return None
138	
139	    # Choose the field to render. Default: the first declared output name (its field_id == name).
140	    field_id = render_field or (spec.outputs[0].name if spec.outputs else "")
141	    disp = result.display_value(field_id)
142	    if not field_id or disp is None:
143	        _disclose(
144	            workspace,
145	            f"verified_compute: field {field_id!r} produced no display_value — no number rendered",
146	        )
147	        return None
148	
149	    # REGISTER: this is what makes the calc token verifiable downstream. Keyed identically to
150	    # strict_verify's consumption ((model_id, spec_hash)).
151	    registry = _ensure_registry(workspace)
152	    registry[result.key()] = result
153	
154	    token = result.calc_token(field_id)
155	    formula = ""
156	    out = spec.output_by_name(field_id.split("@", 1)[0])
157	    if out is not None:
158	        formula = out.formula
159	
160	    _disclose(
161	        workspace,
162	        f"verified_compute: RENDER-ELIGIBLE {disp} via {token} "
163	        f"(formula={formula!r}, inputs={[s.name for s in spec.sourced_inputs]}) — "
164	        "renders ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane, never [#ev:]/[CITE:]",
165	    )
166	
167	    return ComputedClaim(
168	        model_id=result.model_id,
169	        spec_hash=result.spec_hash,
170	        field_id=field_id,
171	        display_value=disp,
172	        calc_token=token,
173	        question=question,
174	        formula=formula,
175	    )
176	
177	
178	def _ensure_registry(workspace: Any) -> dict:
179	    reg = getattr(workspace, "quantified_models", None)
180	    if not isinstance(reg, dict):
181	        reg = {}
182	        try:
183	            workspace.quantified_models = reg
184	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a frozen/odd workspace still gets a usable local registry
185	            pass
186	    return reg
187	
188	
189	def _disclose(workspace: Any, text: str) -> None:
190	    fn = getattr(workspace, "disclose", None)
191	    if callable(fn):
192	        fn(text)
193	    else:
194	        logger.info("[verified_compute] %s", text)
195

def verify_modeled_atom(
    sentence: str,
    calc_match: "re.Match[str]",
    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any],
    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
) -> SentenceVerification:
    """Verify a sentence carrying a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token.

    Fail-closed checks (brief §1.5):
      (a) (model_id, spec_hash) resolves in the RUN-SCOPED registry — a stale or
          foreign model reference fails (P7-19).
      (b) the field exists in that model's executed result.
      (c) the rendered number IMMEDIATELY before the token equals the field's
          canonical ``display_value`` — exact display-string match OR a tight
          numeric backstop (D2). Adjacency means a modeled-assumption number
          elsewhere in the sentence cannot satisfy the token (P7-20).
      (d) every modeled input USED by the field is labeled "(modeled assumption)"
          in the sentence (P7-7/P7-8).
      (e) the field's sourced inputs all resolve in the evidence pool.

    On PASS, returns SentenceVerification whose ``tokens`` are the source-input
    ProvenanceTokens (so resolve_provenance_to_citations cites the inputs) — the
    calc token is stripped downstream. On any failure → is_verified=False (the
    whole sentence is dropped).
    """
    model_id = calc_match.group("model_id")
    spec_hash = calc_match.group("spec_hash")
    field_id = calc_match.group("field")
    failures: list[str] = []

    result = quantified_models.get((model_id, spec_hash))
    if result is None:
        return SentenceVerification(
            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
            failure_reasons=[f"calc_model_not_in_registry:{model_id}:{spec_hash}"],
        )
    fld = getattr(result, "fields", {}).get(field_id)
    if not isinstance(fld, dict):
        return SentenceVerification(
            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
            failure_reasons=[f"calc_field_unknown:{field_id}"],
        )

    display_value = str(fld.get("display_value", ""))

    # (c) adjacency + equality (Codex diff-gate P1-1/P1-2). The rendered number
    # IMMEDIATELY before the token must canonicalize to EXACTLY the field's
    # display_value. Two fail-closed properties:
    #   - FULL-number compare (not `before.endswith(display_value)`, which would
    #     accept "123.40%" for a "23.40%" field — the wrong number ends with the
    #     canonical string).
    #   - RE-CANONICALIZE the parsed adjacent value through the SAME pinned
    #     formatter and require an exact string match (no magnitude-scaled rel-tol
    #     drift, which would accept "$1,000,000,000,999.00" for a
    #     "$1,000,000,000,000.00" field). A benign reformat (missing $/commas) that
    #     canonicalizes to the same string still passes; any real numeric
    #     difference fails.
    before = sentence[: calc_match.start()]
    adj = _CALC_ADJACENT_NUMBER_RE.search(before)
    if adj is None:
        failures.append("calc_no_adjacent_number")
    else:
        adj_str = adj.group(1).strip()
        ok = adj_str == display_value
        if not ok:
            adj_val = _calc_parse_number(adj_str)
            if adj_val is not None:
                from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (
                    _canonical_display,
                )
                recanon = _canonical_display(
                    adj_val,
                    str(fld.get("unit", "")),
                    str(fld.get("display_kind", "number")),
                )
                ok = recanon == display_value
        if not ok:
            failures.append(f"calc_number_mismatch:{adj_str}!={display_value}")

    # (d) modeled-assumption labeling
    modeled_used = fld.get("modeled_used") or []
    if modeled_used and _MODELED_ASSUMPTION_LABEL not in sentence:
        failures.append(
            "calc_modeled_assumption_unlabeled:" + ",".join(map(str, modeled_used))
        )

    # (e) sourced-input evidence resolution + ProvenanceToken construction
    src_tokens: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
    for t in (fld.get("sourced_tokens") or []):
        ev_id = str(t.get("ev_id", ""))
        if ev_id not in evidence_pool:
            failures.append(f"calc_input_ev_not_in_pool:{ev_id}")
            continue
        src_tokens.append(ProvenanceToken(
            evidence_id=ev_id,
            start=int(t.get("start", 0)),
            end=int(t.get("end", 0)),
            raw=str(t.get("raw", "")),
        ))

    if failures:
        return SentenceVerification(
            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
            failure_reasons=failures,
        )
    return SentenceVerification(

            spec.model_id,

    report = strict_verify(
        bound, evidence_pool, quantified_models={result.key(): result},
    )
    telem["verified_sentences"] = report.total_kept
    telem["dropped_sentences"] = report.total_dropped
    if report.total_kept == 0:
        # The spec executed but EVERY computed sentence failed Regime C (e.g. an
        # unlabeled modeled assumption, a number≠display mismatch). No verified prose.
        telem["firing_status"] = "no_verified_sentences"
        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): the spec executed but EVERY computed sentence failed
        # Regime C — this is the faithfulness gate doing its job (a legitimate,
        # NON-broken decline-to-emit). Class it as declined_no_spec (the
        # differentiator did not silently break; it correctly emitted nothing).
        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC)
        logger.warning(
            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (no_verified_sentences): %d computed "
            "sentence(s) all failed Regime C verification (model %s)",
    # "## Evidence base" header + leading "N. " display numbers so strict_verify sees clean span
    # sentences; the [ev_id] markers resolve to real [#ev:...] provenance tokens exactly like the FIX-K
    # draft. Each surviving sentence is a strict_verify-VERIFIED span carrying a real provenance token
    # -> a genuine verified claim the downstream 4-role D8 gate judges (native_gate_b_inputs reads
    # SectionResult.kept_sentences_pre_resolve), NOT a line shipping outside strict_verify/D8.
    # §-1.3: SURFACE the already-uncapped keep-all SUPPORTS set; a line that CANNOT ground is DROPPED
    # by the frozen gate (never padded, never fabricated). The frozen engine is UNTOUCHED (called, not
    # edited). Default-ON via the flag checked inside build_evidence_base_section.
    _own_header = f"## {_EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE}\n\n"
    draft = block[len(_own_header):] if block.startswith(_own_header) else block
    draft = re.sub(r"(?m)^\s*\d+\.\s+", "", draft)  # drop the display "N. " prefixes -> clean span lines
    if not draft.strip():
        return False

    rewritten, _converted, _unverified = _rewrite_draft_with_spans(draft, evidence_pool)
    report = strict_verify(rewritten, evidence_pool)
    kept_verified = [v for v in (report.kept_sentences or []) if getattr(v, "is_verified", False)]
    if not kept_verified:
        return False  # nothing survived the frozen gate -> NO section (no unverified breadth ships)

    local_text, local_biblio, emitted = resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count(
        report.kept_sentences, evidence_pool,
    )
    if emitted <= 0 or not local_text.strip():
        return False

for p in workspace.outline_draft:
        title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "")
        if title.strip().lower() == sec_l or (sec_l and sec_l in title.strip().lower()):
            match = p
            break
    if match is None:
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="preview_section_evidence",
            markdown=f"No section matching {sec!r}.", error="section_not_found",
        )
    title = str(_plan_field(match, "title", "") or "")
    ev_ids = list(_plan_field(match, "ev_ids", []) or [])
    lines = [f"**Section {title!r}**: {len(ev_ids)} assigned ev row(s)", ""]
    for eid in ev_ids[:30]:
        row = workspace.ev_store.get(eid, {})
        gist = (str(row.get("title") or "") or _row_text(row)[:80]).strip()
        lines.append(f"- {eid}: {gist[:120]} [CITE:{eid}]")
    return ToolResult(
        success=True, tool_name="preview_section_evidence", markdown="\n".join(lines),
        source_evidence_ids=ev_ids,
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# verified_compute — THE moat tool: derive a number that renders via [#calc:]
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async def _tool_verified_compute(
    workspace: Any, *, question: str = "", datapoints: Any = None, spec: Any = None,
    render_field: Optional[str] = None, lead: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
) -> ToolResult:
    """Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane (build_quantified_spec -> execute_quantified_model)
    and return a render-eligible sentence carrying its ``[#calc:]`` token — the ONLY compute output
    allowed to render. Fail-closed: a bad spec yields success=False and renders nothing."""
    from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import run_verified_compute  # noqa: PLC0415

    if not isinstance(datapoints, list) or not isinstance(spec, dict):
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
            markdown="verified_compute requires `datapoints` (list) and `spec` (dict).",
            error="bad_args",
        )
    claim = await run_verified_compute(
        workspace, question=str(question or workspace.research_question),
        datapoints=datapoints, raw_spec=spec, render_field=render_field,
    )
    if claim is None:
        return ToolResult(
            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
            markdown="verified_compute: spec failed to build/execute (fail-closed) — no number rendered.",
            error="spec_rejected_or_exec_failed",
        )
    lead_text = str(lead or "").strip() or "The computed value is"
    sentence = claim.render_sentence(lead_text)
    return ToolResult(
        success=True, tool_name="verified_compute",
        markdown=(
            f"VERIFIED computed value **{claim.display_value}** renders via `{claim.calc_token}`.\n\n"
            f"Render-ready sentence: {sentence}"
        ),
        statistics={
            "display_value": claim.display_value, "model_id": claim.model_id,
            "spec_hash": claim.spec_hash, "field_id": claim.field_id,
            "calc_token": claim.calc_token, "formula": claim.formula,
            "render_sentence": sentence,
        },
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# helpers + registration
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _assigned_ev_ids(workspace: Any) -> set[str]:
    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
    return {
        eid
        for p in workspace.outline_draft
        for eid in (_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", None) or [])
    }


def register_outline_toolkit(
    registry: ToolRegistry,
    workspace: Any,
    agent_model: str,
    deadline: Optional[float] = None,
) -> list[str]:
    """Wire the T1 read-only/deterministic tools + verified_compute onto ``registry``. Returns the
    list of registered tool names. The driver iterates the registry unchanged."""

    def _bind(fn):
        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
            return await fn(workspace, **kw)
        return _exec

    specs = [
        ("calculator", _tool_calculator,
         "Evaluate ONE arithmetic expression (deterministic, no LLM). EXPLORATORY — render a "
         "derived number via verified_compute, not this.",
         {"expression": "a pure arithmetic expression, e.g. (1493602-903095)*1000"}),
        ("get_evidence", _tool_get_evidence,
         "Return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by id.",
         {"ev_id": "the evidence id", "max_chars": "optional text cap (default 4000)"}),
        ("search_corpus", _tool_search_corpus,
         "Keyword search over the evidence ALREADY in the pool (no network). The 'do we already "
         "have this?' stop before any live fetch.",
         {"query": "keywords to search", "top_k": "max hits (default 8)"}),
        ("list_baskets", _tool_list_baskets,
         "One line per basket (id, members, corroboration, assignment) — scan the whole corpus "
         "shape at once.",
         {}),
        ("coverage_audit", _tool_coverage_audit,
         "Deterministic coverage accounting: unassigned baskets, per-section counts, residual "
         "fraction, sections below floor.",
         {}),
        ("preview_section_evidence", _tool_preview_section_evidence,
         "Preview one section's assigned evidence — exactly what compose will receive.",
         {"section": "the section title"}),
        ("verified_compute", _tool_verified_compute,
         "Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane and render it via a [#calc:] token (the ONLY "
         "compute output allowed to render).",
         {"question": "what is being computed",
          "datapoints": "list of sourced datapoints {evidence_id,label,context,value,unit}",
          "spec": "the model spec {model_id,title,inputs,outputs}",
          "render_field": "optional output field to render (default first)",
          "lead": "optional lead prose for the rendered sentence"}),
    ]
    registered: list[str] = []
    for name, fn, desc, params in specs:
        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
            name=name, description=desc, requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
            parameters=params, execute=_bind(fn),
        ))
        registered.append(name)
    return registered

1	"""W3-render: the compute-reachability P1, render half — CLOSED and PROVED end-to-end.
2	
3	This is the load-bearing faithfulness proof for the agentic outliner's moat (COMPUTE-and-PROVE
4	numbers). It exercises the REAL machinery — no mocks of the verifier, spec builder, sandbox, or
5	canonical formatter — on ONE real FinanceBench case (Adobe operating income change
6	FY2015->FY2016, gold delta ≈ $590 million).
7	
8	Two invariants, proven together:
9	  (A) RENDER LANE: a number the agent DERIVED ($590,507,000.00 = (1,493,602 - 903,095) * 1000
10	      thousands USD) renders through ``strict_verify`` via its ``[#calc:...]`` token —
11	      kept=1, dropped=0 — and the exact canonical display string survives.
12	  (D) DROP GUARD: the SAME derived number, routed instead through the ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE:...]``
13	      evidence-span path, is DROPPED (``number_not_in_any_cited_span``) — because a derived value
14	      appears in no single source span. This is the faithfulness invariant: a derived/unverified
15	      number can NEVER reach the citation render path (a P0 breach if it could).
16	
17	REAL DATA: the evidence spans carry the actual 10-K operating-income literals; the span is
18	DERIVED by the tool (``literal_span_is_faithful``), not asserted by the test.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import asyncio
23	
24	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import strict_verify
25	from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import OutlineWorkspace
26	from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import ComputedClaim, run_verified_compute
27	
28	# Adobe 10-K, thousands USD: FY2016 operating income = 1,493,602 ; FY2015 = 903,095.
29	# delta = 590,507 (thousands) = $590,507,000  (FinanceBench gold ≈ $590 million).
30	_EV = {
31	    "ev_2016": {
32	        "evidence_id": "ev_2016",
33	        "direct_quote": "Adobe reported operating income of 1,493,602 for fiscal 2016.",
34	    },
35	    "ev_2015": {
36	        "evidence_id": "ev_2015",
37	        "direct_quote": "Adobe reported operating income of 903,095 for fiscal 2015.",
38	    },
39	}
40	_DATAPOINTS = [
41	    {"evidence_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
42	     "value": "1493602", "unit": "kUSD"},
43	    {"evidence_id": "ev_2015", "label": "opinc_2015", "context": "fiscal 2015",
44	     "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"},
45	]
46	_RAW_SPEC = {
47	    "model_id": "opinc_delta",
48	    "title": "Adobe operating income change FY2015->FY2016",
49	    "inputs": [
50	        {"name": "opinc_2016", "datapoint_ref": {
51	            "ev_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
52	            "value": "1493602", "unit": "kUSD"}},
53	        {"name": "opinc_2015", "datapoint_ref": {
54	            "ev_id": "ev_2015", "label": "opinc_2015", "context": "fiscal 2015",
55	            "value": "903095", "unit": "kUSD"}},
56	    ],
57	    # *1000 converts the thousands-denominated literals to dollars; the scale is a formula
58	    # constant (disclosed), the INPUT literals stay verbatim-cited.
59	    "outputs": [
60	        {"name": "delta", "formula": "(opinc_2016 - opinc_2015) * 1000",
61	         "unit": "USD", "display_kind": "currency"},
62	    ],
63	}
64	_QUESTION = "What was Adobe's operating income change from FY2015 to FY2016?"
65	_GOLD_DISPLAY = "$590,507,000.00"
66	
67	
68	def _workspace() -> OutlineWorkspace:
69	    return OutlineWorkspace(research_question=_QUESTION, ev_store=dict(_EV))
70	
71	
72	def _compute(ws: OutlineWorkspace) -> ComputedClaim:
73	    claim = asyncio.run(run_verified_compute(
74	        ws, question=_QUESTION, datapoints=_DATAPOINTS, raw_spec=_RAW_SPEC,
75	    ))
76	    assert claim is not None, "verified_compute returned None — the Adobe spec must build+execute"
77	    return claim
78	
79	
80	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- build/register
81	
82	
83	def test_verified_compute_produces_gold_display_and_registers_model():
84	    ws = _workspace()
85	    claim = _compute(ws)
86	
87	    assert claim.display_value == _GOLD_DISPLAY, (
88	        f"gold delta must be {_GOLD_DISPLAY}, got {claim.display_value}"
89	    )
90	    assert claim.model_id == "opinc_delta"
91	    assert claim.field_id == "delta"
92	    assert claim.calc_token == f"[#calc:opinc_delta:{claim.spec_hash}:delta]"
93	    # The verified model is registered EXACTLY as strict_verify consumes it.
94	    assert (claim.model_id, claim.spec_hash) in ws.quantified_models
95	    # ...and this is a SEPARATE surface from exploratory execute_python output.
96	    assert ws.compute_results == []
97	
98	
99	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (A) render lane
100	
101	
102	def test_derived_number_RENDERS_through_the_calc_lane_kept1_dropped0():
103	    ws = _workspace()
104	    claim = _compute(ws)
105	
106	    sentence = claim.render_sentence("Adobe's operating income rose by")
107	    assert _GOLD_DISPLAY in sentence and claim.calc_token in sentence
108	
109	    report = strict_verify(sentence, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)
110	
111	    assert report.total_kept == 1 and report.total_dropped == 0, (
112	        f"CALC LANE must keep the computed sentence, got "
113	        f"kept={report.total_kept} dropped={report.total_dropped} "
114	        f"reasons={[d.failure_reasons for d in report.dropped_sentences]}"
115	    )
116	    kept = report.kept_sentences[0]
117	    # The kept sentence's provenance tokens are the SOURCE INPUTS (the calc token strips to the
118	    # inputs it was derived from), so the rendered number is traceable to the two 10-K spans.
119	    cited = {t.evidence_id for t in kept.tokens}
120	    assert cited == {"ev_2016", "ev_2015"}, f"computed number must cite BOTH inputs, got {cited}"
121	
122	
123	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (D) drop guard
124	
125	
126	def test_derived_number_via_CITE_evidence_span_is_DROPPED():
127	    """The faithfulness invariant: a derived number can NEVER render through [#ev:]/[CITE:].
128	
129	    Route the SAME $590,507,000.00 through an evidence-span citation over a VALID in-bounds span
130	    of a real source. It is dropped because the derived digits appear in no single source span.
131	    """
132	    ws = _workspace()
133	    claim = _compute(ws)
134	
135	    span_len = len(_EV["ev_2016"]["direct_quote"])
136	    bad = f"Adobe's operating income rose by {claim.display_value} [#ev:ev_2016:0-{span_len}]."
137	
138	    report = strict_verify(bad, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)
139	
140	    assert report.total_kept == 0 and report.total_dropped == 1, (
141	        f"a derived number cited via an evidence span MUST be dropped, got "
142	        f"kept={report.total_kept} dropped={report.total_dropped}"
143	    )
144	    reasons = report.dropped_sentences[0].failure_reasons
145	    assert any("number_not_in_any_cited_span" in r for r in reasons), (
146	        f"drop must be the number-not-in-span faithfulness guard, got {reasons}"
147	    )
148	
149	
150	def test_a_wrong_number_next_to_the_calc_token_is_DROPPED():
151	    """Regime-C adjacency (check c): the calc token cannot be reused to bless a DIFFERENT number.
152	
153	    If the outline text places a wrong figure immediately before the (valid, registered) calc
154	    token, strict_verify drops it — the token proves ONLY its own canonical display value.
155	    """
156	    ws = _workspace()
157	    claim = _compute(ws)
158	
159	    wrong = f"Adobe's operating income rose by $999,999,999.00 {claim.calc_token}."
160	    report = strict_verify(wrong, ws.ev_store, quantified_models=ws.quantified_models)
161	
162	    assert report.total_kept == 0 and report.total_dropped == 1, (
163	        "a wrong number adjacent to a real calc token must be dropped"
164	    )
165	    reasons = report.dropped_sentences[0].failure_reasons
166	    assert any("calc_number_mismatch" in r for r in reasons), reasons
167	
168	
169	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fail-closed build
170	
171	
172	def test_unfaithful_literal_span_fails_closed_and_renders_nothing():
173	    """If the claimed input value is NOT present in the cited evidence, no model is built and no
174	    number becomes render-eligible (fail-closed). The registry stays empty."""
175	    ws = _workspace()
176	    bad_dps = [
177	        # value 9999999 is nowhere in ev_2016's text -> no unique literal span.
178	        {"evidence_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
179	         "value": "9999999", "unit": "kUSD"},
180	        _DATAPOINTS[1],
181	    ]
182	    bad_spec = dict(_RAW_SPEC)
183	    bad_spec["inputs"] = [
184	        {"name": "opinc_2016", "datapoint_ref": {
185	            "ev_id": "ev_2016", "label": "opinc_2016", "context": "fiscal 2016",
186	            "value": "9999999", "unit": "kUSD"}},
187	        _RAW_SPEC["inputs"][1],
188	    ]
189	
190	    claim = asyncio.run(run_verified_compute(
191	        ws, question=_QUESTION, datapoints=bad_dps, raw_spec=bad_spec,
192	    ))
193	    assert claim is None, "an unfaithful input must fail-closed (no render-eligible number)"
194	    assert ws.quantified_models == {}, "no model may be registered on a fail-closed build"
195	    assert any("REJECTED" in d for d in ws.disclosures), "the reject must be disclosed"
196

input.gap_to_top: Metric (c) is at zero: no T2 battery exists (scripts/outline_battery.py unbuilt), so S0/S1 cannot be measured, let alone trend to zero. Metric (b) is partial: fetch_url, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile, and the CORE-set/ToolDefinition-tags scaling are missing. And a production seam gap I verified directly: NO production strict_verify call receives workspace.quantified_models — multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 call strict_verify(text, evidence_pool) with quantified_models=None, which skips the Regime-C calc router, so an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence would be fail-closed DROPPED in a real full-corpus run (faithfulness-safe but the moat number never ships; the end-to-end proof only passes the registry explicitly inside the test/probe). Also no full-corpus cp4_used=agentic re-run has been measured with the new toolkit active.
input.next_step: Build the T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures per the redesign doc) as the measurement instrument for metric (c), and include: (i) S0 calc-lane cases including a wrong-pair-subtraction semantic case (DEFER the mirror-model question<->formula entailment check — fail-open LLM checks cannot strengthen a fail-closed gate; add it only if this battery case shows the mode fires); (ii) one integration case that drives an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence + workspace.quantified_models through the DOWNSTREAM strict_verify handoff — this will expose and force the fix that multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 never receive the registry (plumb workspace.quantified_models into those calls so the computed number actually ships in production, not just in the probe); (iii) H07/H22 contradiction cases marked xfail until find_contradictions lands. Build fetch_url immediately after (trivial now via fold_in_fetched_rows at _fold_in.py:150), then find_contradictions/corroboration_profile to flip H07/H22 from xfail to measured.
input.reason: The round's diff is correct and honestly measured: I independently re-ran 46/46 tests, confirmed the Adobe $590,507,000.00 calc-lane render (kept=1, both input spans cited) and all three drop-guards use the real verifier with no mocks, and the fold-in seam refactor is behavior-preserving with fail-loud id-collision. Faithfulness invariants hold — exploratory execute_python output stays renderable=False and cannot enter quantified_models, and a derived number structurally cannot pass the [CITE:]/[#ev:] span path. But the bar is the benchmark, not the bug: metric (c) is unmeasured (no battery), metric (b) is partial, and the production quantified_models handoff seam is unwired, so the moat number ships only in tests today. CONTINUE.
